experience.title: Director Of Sales Marketing
experience.description: Part of Edwardian Hotels London. The May Fair Hotel: There are 5 star luxury hotels, and then there is The May Fair: an icon of expressive contemporary design, bringing together boutique attention to detail with grand hotel service. A legendary luxury hotel with a glamorous past, The May Fair was first opened by King George V in 1927 and offers 404 luxury hotel bedrooms, including 12 of the capital's most memorable hotel suites, a discreet spa, a fine-dining restaurant and the quintessential London Casino. The spirit of surrounding Mayfair is captured beautifully, especially in our destination May Fair Bar, breath taking Crystal Room and opulent Danziger Suite, which between them have played host to some of London's most extravagant events.

experience.title: General Manager
experience.description: As the General Manager of two of London’s finest boutique hotels I am responsible for all hotel operations by proving and implementing developed strategies and services. I work very closely with my head of departments, team and directors of Edwardian Hotels London. Within this responsibility I have to account for needs of our owners, employees and our valued guests. We develop and implement business strategies for the hotel in accordance with the company’s philosophy and standards. My aim is to maximize revenue by developing and implementing sales, marketing, booking and revenue plans.

linkedin_profile.about: With over 15 years in the hospitality industry, my core competencies lie in strategic business development and fostering key accounts within assigned markets. At Edwardian Hotels London, I am committed to nurturing high-end programs and partnerships that resonate with our brand's luxury experience. My goal is to continue driving growth and excellence within our organisation by exploring diverse perspectives utilising a deep understanding of the luxury travel sector. In my current role, I am responsible for the sales strategy of three luxury properties, focusing on various market segments including leisure groups, MICE, corporates, and online travel companies. My expertise in customer relations and my proven track record in the hospitality industry have been instrumental in developing and sustaining successful partnerships, ultimately contributing to the robust performance and prestige of our hotel portfolio.

linkedin_profile.headline: EVP | Commercial Director at Edwardian Hotels London - The Londoner, The May Fair, The Edwardian Manchester
linkedin_profile.about: Hasnain is proud to serve as the Commercial Director of Edwardian Hotels London, one of the largest privately-owned hospitality companies in the UK. A highly experienced hotelier, he has successfully worked across a range of roles within the sector during a career spanning more than two decades. An exemplary project manager, with a proven track-record of managing large teams, he started his current role in 2016 and is responsible for developing and implementing commercial strategies to accelerate growth and revenue across the group. He is devoted to understanding the expectations of guests to ensure that their needs are met at all times and inspires his diverse group of teams to create and provide exceptional experiences for anyone who walks through their hotel doors. Part of this work includes collaborating with all disciplines including the hospitality group’s in-house tech team to develop innovative services to improve customer experience. As a leader in the business, Hasnain has been instrumental in planning for The Londoner, the latest project by Edwardian Hotels London, set to open its doors late 2020 as the world’s first Super Boutique hotel in London’s Leicester Square. Hasnain is an avid user of social media, recognising its benefits of engaging with customers one-on-one as well as at scale. He also finds it benefits his understanding of key trends and traveller behaviours. In his personal time, Hasnain is committed to volunteering at Age UK.

experience.title: Learning And Development Coordinator
experience.description: Continuously update training materials for existing courses and manage the development of new material. Schedule classes, based on availability of classrooms, equipment and instructors. Lead Trainings. Collaborate with HR Manager on implementation of Learning & Development programs.

experience.title: Graduate Management Trainee
experience.description: The Graduate Management Programme is an 18 months fast track Future Leaders Programme designed to provide to the Management Trainee an exceptional and complete overview of hospitality and of the hotel operation. The first three months of the Programme offer a cross training in F&B, C&B, Rooms, S&M, Reservations, Revenue, Finance, HR and Customer Service across all the luxury hotels in part of The Edwardian Hotels London. At the end of the three months there is the opportunity for the Graduate Management Trainee to express her preference and to be allocated to the position and department she is interested in.

experience.title: Reception Guest Relations Team Leader
experience.location: Radisson Blu Edwardian Mercer Street Hotel, London
experience.description: The Guest Service Team Leader role oversees daily operation of the Reception of the Hotel. The daily tasks are: Make sure that staff are highly trained, motivated and supervised; Delegate different customer service duties to team members to ensure a faster and smoother flow of operation through division of labor; Take care of the communication between the Night Manager and Assistant Managers, following up on guest requests and problems; Create better modes of operations to make customer service easier for both team members and customers; Ensure that all equipment and appliances needed by the customer team members are available and in good condition to enable smooth flow of operations and customer satisfactions; Analyse customer feedback and provide strategic direction to continuously improve overall rating; Actively listen and resolve guests’ complaints; Oversee and coordinate all arrivals and departures of VIPs guests; Promote all amenities, conveniences and programs offered; Be part of the Duty Managers Team of the Hotel; To assist on cover for night shift if needed.

experience.title: Guest Relations Manager
experience.description: Managing all aspects of the guest relations department. Building rapport with all the guests, to effectively resolve guest complaints in a timely manner and to act as a liaison for The May Fair Hotel's VIP guests to ensure that their expectations are exceeded at every opportunity. Acting as a duty manager for the hotel. Assisting FOHM in analysing Medallia feedback and putting action plans together to improve guest satisfaction. Assisting FOHM to analysing all feedback from guests identify trends and assist departments in improvement plans. Ensuring appropriate professional standards of behaviour, work standards, productivity, practices and customer care are enforced. Training of Front Office staff appropriately and proactively.

experience.title: Reception Manager
experience.description: To lead the operation of service excellence in the Concierge, Guest Relations and Reception to establish a winning and motivated guest service team and to deliver a great service experience which makes people feel special. To help to handle all aspects of Concierge, Guest Relations and Reception and to maintain upkeep and appearance of Hotel lobby and entrance. To train, o coach and to recognize staff. To research local area facilities, this may be of help and interest to guests. To establish working relations with relevant ticket agencies in order to provide a comprehensive Concierge service to guests. To ensure that guest service employees are up-to-date with local area knowledge. To cover Duty Manager shifts.

experience.title: Assistant Front Of House Manager
experience.description: To be accountable for overseeing the smooth operations of the Front Office department. To work alongside side the Front Office Manager and run the Front Office department effectively by ensuring maximum guest satisfaction, departmental profitability, achieving monthly goals and adhering to the standard required by the hotel. Maintain excellent communication with other hotel departments. Perform all tasks of a Front Office Staff as needed to facilitate service. Ensure that the Front Desk is appropriately staffed according to activity levels. Ensures that the front desk is aware of all new promotions and changes. Analyse, investigate, and resolve guests complaints.

experience.title: Group Reservations Manager
experience.description: To manage the daily operations and efficient running of the Central Reservations Department of Edwardian Hotels London and our portfolio of 12 luxury hotels. Provide direction and leadership to Reservations team assisting them to achieve their potential and achieve overall departmental objectives. To drive the team to achieve goals (Revenue and Quality) and develop new ideas to improve processes To demonstrate an in depth knowledge and understanding of Hotel systems to include and not limited to PMS, CRM, SalesForce To ensure all administrative procedures are completed accurately and in accordance with the departmental and Hotel guidelines To ensure accurate, up to date contract information, rate agreements, data input into Opera Reservation System, for both existing and new clients To ensure that all accounting procedures are adhered to in conjunction with the Revenue Managers. To ensure together with the revenue team that all free sale and inventory are being kept up to date.

experience.title: Group Guest Experience Manager
experience.description: To manage the operation and efficient running of the Central Guest Services department of Edwardian Hotels London and our portfolio of 12 luxury hotels. To inspire, lead and enable the team to provide seamless and unparalleled service whilst maintaining profitability and employee satisfaction. To ensure the team is kept updated with any promotions, changes or product enhancements to guarantee an up-to-date product knowledge of hotels portfolio. Key point of contact between the Guest Service team and finance team, ensuring financial compliance along all related process completed by the service team. Administrative support including accidents and incidents reports communicating and liaising with insurance companies as well as internal H&S and security departments. To monitor guest feedback, communication and continuously communicates with the front office teams to enhance guests' experience. To create an environment where the team is satisfied and continuously motivated by actively seeking feedback and make improvements and changes.

linkedin_profile.headline: Director of Sales Middle East & Diplomatic Market for Edwardian Hotels (The May Fair Hotel, The Londoner & The Edwardian Manchester Hotel) | Driving Revenue Growth & Global Market Expansion

experience.title: Sales Director Middle East And Diplomatic Market - For Edwardian Hotels (14 Hotels)
experience.description: Director of Sales for the Middle East and Diplomatic market for the MayFair hotel, The Londoner & The Edwardian Manchester hotel, London. I have the privilege of championing 14 luxury properties, including The May Fair, The Londoner, and The Edwardian Manchester. My role goes beyond driving revenue, it’s about building meaningful relationships, understanding the cultural nuances of our Middle Eastern clientele, and delivering tailored experiences that inspire loyalty. I lead market strategies that open new doors, nurture partnerships with embassies and diplomatic missions, and craft bespoke campaigns that elevate our brand on the global stage. Whether representing our hotels at international trade shows or collaborating with internal teams to design high-impact sales initiatives, I thrive on connecting people, creating opportunities, and turning our guests’ expectations into lasting memories.
